reasoning: The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" shows an empty cell (no vaccine) for Human ewingii ehrlichiosis as of October 2025. Empirical base rates for developing a new vaccine within a few months are basically 0. Furthermore, this is a relatively rare tick-borne bacterial disease that is readily treated with doxycycline, meaning there is little commercial or public health pressure driving final-stage vaccine trials. Searches indicate no candidate vaccines in advanced trials that could foreseeably be approved by early 2026. Therefore, the probability that a vaccine will be developed and listed on Wikipedia by the resolution dates is overwhelmingly low. I am setting the probabilities at their minimum bounds, rising slightly for later dates to account for general uncertainty increasing with horizon.
